money_flow_summary: Enterprises and developers pay AI platform providers via API usage. Solo operators sit in the middle: building AI-powered products that capture value between commodity APIs and business users.

opportunities.title: AI Voice Agent for Inbound SMB Calls (Listed in SaaS — primary entry point)
opportunities.how_money_is_made: See SaaS industry entry — this is the #1 opportunity across all 28 industries.
opportunities.first_step: Vapi free account → dental booking agent → demo in 1 day
opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI IS the product — voice agent replaces human receptionist at 1/10 the cost

competitors.name: Ruby / Smith.ai
competitors.weakness: Human-powered; $250–$1,000+/mo; limited hours; can't scale to 100 simultaneous calls

opportunities.gap: Fully automated voice AI for specific verticals at SMB pricing ($199–$999/mo) vs. human services at $500–$2,000/mo
poc_steps: Build dental booking agent with Google Calendar

opportunities.title: AI QA Testing Agent for Web & Mobile Apps
opportunities.how_money_is_made: AI agent explores app autonomously like a QA tester, identifies bugs, generates structured bug reports with screenshots and reproduction steps. Charge dev teams $99–$499/mo. Replaces 1–2 days of manual QA per sprint.
opportunities.moat: CI/CD integration creates workflow dependency; test history database compounds
opportunities.first_step: Build Playwright-based AI agent using Claude's computer use API; test on 5 open-source apps; post demo video on Twitter/X
opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI explores the UI like a human tester — clicking flows, testing edge cases, finding usability bugs

competitors.name: Playwright / Selenium
competitors.weakness: Requires humans to write test scripts; not autonomous; breaks on UI changes; no bug reporting

competitors.name: Testim
competitors.weakness: $450+/mo; still requires test case definition; not truly autonomous exploration

competitors.name: Mabl
competitors.weakness: Enterprise ($600+/mo); requires setup; not truly autonomous; focused on regression

opportunities.gap: Existing QA tools need humans to define test scripts. An AI that autonomously explores an app and finds bugs without predefined test cases — like a human exploratory QA tester — doesn't exist at affordable prices.
poc_steps: Write agent that opens a URL and clicks through 20 random UI elements
Log any errors, slow loads, or broken flows
Show 1 dev team: 'here are 8 bugs your QA team missed'

opportunities.title: Computer Vision Inventory Counter for Restaurants/Retail
opportunities.how_money_is_made: Employee walks storeroom with phone, takes 30-sec video, AI counts every item, compares to par levels, generates purchase order. Charge $149–$499/mo. Targets 1M+ restaurants and 3M+ small retailers.
opportunities.moat: Custom product catalog per business creates switching cost; accuracy improves with more training data
opportunities.first_step: Build mobile video capture + YOLO/Vision API item counting; test with 1 local restaurant; produce ROI calculator
opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI recognizes thousands of products from video in seconds — replacing 2+ hours of manual counting

competitors.name: Crunchtime (restaurant inventory)
competitors.weakness: Enterprise ($1k+/mo); manual counting still required; just a system of record, not automation

competitors.name: BlueCart
competitors.weakness: Ordering platform; no computer vision; requires manual count entry

competitors.name: None at SMB price point
competitors.weakness: CV inventory counting for restaurants doesn't exist affordably

opportunities.gap: No affordable mobile tool auto-counts restaurant inventory from video. Manual inventory takes 2–4 hours/week per location. This is a solved technical problem (YOLO/Vision API) that no SMB-priced product has deployed.
poc_steps: Use Google Vision API or Roboflow to detect objects in a photo
Go to a friend's restaurant/bar, photograph the beer walk-in
Show: 'AI counted 47 Modelo, 23 Corona, 15 Blue Moon'
